perturbation.original_text: Unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, the "Term" of this Agreement shall consist of, collectively, the Initial Term and the Renewal Term, if applicable. The "Initial Term" shall commence upon the Effective Date and shall expire on March 31, 2011.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, the "Term" of this Agreement shall consist of, collectively, the Initial Term and the Renewal Term, if applicable. The "Initial Term" shall commence upon the Effective Date and shall expire on March 31, 2009.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'March 31, 2011' to 'March 31, 2009' in Section 2(a) introduces an in-text contradiction. This is because Section 2(b) then refers to the expiration date when discussing renewal terms. This creates uncertainty in the event Affiliate doesn't notify Network of its desire that this Agreement terminate at least six (6) months before the expiration date, where it is contradicting with the date being 2009 now instead of 2011, it will automatically renew, upon the same terms and conditions, for an additional four (4)-year period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Renewal Term. If Affiliate fails to notify Network of its desire that this Agreement terminate on its expiration date, at least six (6) months before the expiration date, this Agreement will automatically renew, upon the same terms and conditions, for an additional four (4) -year period ("Renewal Term").
perturbation.justification: The change from 'March 31, 2011' to 'March 31, 2009' in the 'Initial Term' expiration date directly contradicts the automatic renewal clause in Section 2(b). Section 2(b) states that if the Affiliate fails to notify Network of its desire to terminate at least six months before the 'expiration date', the agreement automatically renews. The change alters the 'expiration date', causing a direct conflict regarding when the notification deadline is, creating a clear inconsistency as to when the agreement should be terminated to avoid renewal.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a Station initially listed on Exhibit A does not launch the Service by the Launch Date as required by Section 3(f) other than as a result of a force majeure event pursuant to Section 13(e), Network shall have the right to terminate this Agreement only as to such Station, but if three (3) or more Stations initially listed on Exhibit A do not launch the Service by the pertinent Launch Date for each such Station as required by Section 3(f) for reasons other than force majeure, Network, at its option, shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in its entirety or only as to such Station or Stations.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a Station initially listed on Exhibit A does not launch the Service by the Launch Date as required by Section 3(f) other than as a result of a force majeure event pursuant to Section 13(e), Affiliate shall have the right to terminate this Agreement only as to such Station, but if three (3) or more Stations initially listed on Exhibit A do not launch the Service by the pertinent Launch Date for each such Station as required by Section 3(f) for reasons other than force majeure, Network, at its option, shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in its entirety or only as to such Station or Stations.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Network shall have the right to terminate this Agreement only as to such Station' to 'Affiliate shall have the right to terminate this Agreement only as to such Station' creates an in-text contradiction. It is contradicting in the same section where the text says 'Network, at its option, shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in its entirety or only as to such Station or Stations.', because it changes the rights of the party to be able to terminate a station or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a Station initially listed on Exhibit A does not launch the Service by the Launch Date as required by Section 3(f) other than as a result of a force majeure event pursuant to Section 13(e), Affiliate shall have the right to terminate this Agreement only as to such Station, but if three (3) or more Stations initially listed on Exhibit A do not launch the Service by the pertinent Launch Date for each such Station as required by Section 3(f) for reasons other than force majeure, Network, at its option, shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in its entirety or only as to such Station or Stations. In the event that Network terminates this Agreement as to a particular Station or several Stations, or in its entirety pursuant to Sections 10(a) or (c), Affiliate shall, within thirty (30) days of termination, at its option either reimburse Network for the cost of all equipment or return such equipment related to such Station(s) that was paid for by Network pursuant to Section 5(b) herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Network' to 'Affiliate' having the right to terminate the agreement in the first part of the sentence, while the second part of the sentence states 'Network' has the right to terminate the agreement creates a direct contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. This creates confusion over who has the right to terminate the agreement under the specified circumstances, leading to uncertainty about the enforceability of the clause and potentially leading to disputes.

perturbation.original_text: The Affiliate Advertising Share, if any, shall be payable quarterly and shall be due no later than forty-five (45) days following the end of each calendar quarter for which a payment is due.
perturbation.changed_text: The Affiliate Advertising Share, if any, shall be payable quarterly and shall be due no later than ninety (90) days following the end of each calendar quarter for which a payment is due.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'forty-five (45) days' to 'ninety (90) days' creates an in-text contradiction when considering Section 7(b), which states: Network shall submit to Affiliate, within forty-five (45) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, a statement reporting for such calendar quarter the following information on a Station-by-Station basis: (i) Network's Advertising Revenue, (ii) the Affiliate Advertising Share. If the payment is due in ninety (90) days and the statement is provided in forty-five (45) days, there's an in-text contradiction, because it is uncertain when the payment will be made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Network shall submit to Affiliate, within forty-five (45) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, a statement reporting for such calendar quarter the following information on a Station-by-Station basis: (i) Network's Advertising Revenue, (ii) the Affiliate Advertising Share, (iii) Network's Transactional Revenue, (iv) the average number of households receiving the Service through each MVPD in each DMA served by a Station, as calculated herein, and (v) the Affiliate Transactional Share. If this Agreement terminates on any date other than at the end of a calendar quarter, Network shall supply such statement as of the date of termination, within forty-five (45) days thereafter, and this obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement until Affiliate receives such statement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated payment was due 45 days after the end of each quarter. The changed text extends this to 90 days. However, Section 7(b) requires Network to submit a statement to the Affiliate within 45 days. If the statement is provided in 45 days but payment is not due for 90 days, there's a contradiction regarding the payment timeline. This directly impacts the obligations and creates inconsistency, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
